implementation_notes: Implementation via task-work delegation. Files planned: 0, Files actual: 0
requirements_addressed: AC-001: ALLOWED_BINARIES single source of truth
AC-002: dispatch via DeepAgents execute seam
AC-005: missing-creds path returns None without crashing
requirements_remaining: Optional: tests/integration/test_end_to_end_build.py (opt-in integration test against a throwaway test repo) was not implemented to stay within the minimal documentation file budget; the task spec marks it as opt-in.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Single named constant ALLOWED_BINARIES defined here; TASK-IC-009 imports it (single source of truth)
completion_promises.evidence: Defined ALLOWED_BINARIES = frozenset({'git', 'gh', 'pytest'}) in src/forge/build/git_operations.py with module docstring tying it to TASK-REV-IC8B Risk 5. Updated src/forge/build/test_verification.py to import the constant via `from forge.build.git_operations import ALLOWED_BINARIES as _BINARY_ALLOWLIST` and added _allowed_binaries_for_test() returning the same object (verified by `is` identity check in test_test_verification_imports_same_object).

completion_promises.criterion_text: All four functions invoke via DeepAgents execute tool (not direct subprocess.run())
completion_promises.evidence: All four operations route through `_execute_via_deepagents` seam (the same pattern as TASK-IC-009). The default seam body raises RuntimeError so accidental production use without binding fails loudly. No `subprocess` import in git_operations.py (verified by grep). Tests in TestExecuteSeamRouting confirm dispatch via the seam.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Working directory parameter enforced for every call; the execute tool's working-directory allowlist validates worktree_path is under the configured builds directory
completion_promises.evidence: _run_via_execute() validates worktree_path.is_absolute() before dispatch (raises ValueError otherwise) and forwards str(worktree_path) as the seam's `cwd` argument so the execute tool's working-dir allowlist has a concrete absolute path to validate. Tests test_create_*_dispatches_via_seam and test_relative_worktree_path_rejected_pre_dispatch confirm both branches.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Credentials read from environment only (GH_TOKEN, GITHUB_TOKEN) — NEVER from forge.yaml
completion_promises.evidence: _has_credentials() reads only os.environ.get('GH_TOKEN') / os.environ.get('GITHUB_TOKEN'). No config-file access or YAML parsing in the module. Tokens never appear on argv — test_command_args_never_carry_credentials confirms the seeded GH_TOKEN value does not appear in any argv element passed to the seam. test_github_token_alone_satisfies_check confirms either env var is sufficient.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Missing credentials → create_pull_request() records reason on SessionOutcome and does not crash
completion_promises.evidence: create_pull_request() short-circuits to return None (with WARNING log) when no credentials are in env (test_no_credentials_skips_gh_and_returns_none) — gh is never invoked. If gh is invoked but reports auth failure (per _AUTH_FAILURE_MARKERS), the function logs and returns None instead of raising (test_gh_auth_failure_returns_none). Empty token strings are also treated as missing (test_empty_string_token_treated_as_missing). The 'records reason on SessionOutcome' is the caller's job — the function provides a None return signal for that path. SessionOutcome itself has extra='forbid' so we cannot mutate it directly here without schema changes outside this task's scope.

completion_promises.criterion_text: Attempted invocation of disallowed binary (e.g. rm, curl) raises a clear error from the execute tool layer
completion_promises.evidence: DisallowedBinaryError(ValueError) is raised by _run_via_execute() before the seam is touched when Path(command[0]).name is not in ALLOWED_BINARIES. Tests test_rm_is_rejected, test_curl_is_rejected, test_path_prefixed_disallowed_binary_rejected confirm that rm, curl, and /usr/bin/rm are all rejected; test_path_prefixed_allowed_binary_accepted confirms /usr/local/bin/git still works. Error message includes the offending binary, the sorted allowlist, and the ADR/review requirement.

completion_promises.criterion_text: create_pull_request() returns the PR URL (parsed from gh pr create stdout)
completion_promises.evidence: _parse_pr_url() scans stdout in reverse for the last line starting with http:// or https://. Tests test_pr_url_returned_when_url_is_last_line and test_pr_url_returned_when_only_url_in_stdout confirm the happy path; test_pr_url_none_when_stdout_lacks_url confirms graceful None for the pathological case where gh exits 0 without printing a URL.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All operations are awaitable so the build loop can interleave them
completion_promises.evidence: All four public operations are declared `async def` and TestAwaitable.test_function_is_coroutine_function parametrizes inspect.iscoroutinefunction() over [create_branch, commit_changes, push_branch, create_pull_request] — all four pass.

completion_promises.criterion_text: All modified files pass project-configured lint/format checks with zero errors
completion_promises.evidence: Ran `python3 -m black --target-version py312 --check` over src/forge/build/git_operations.py, src/forge/build/test_verification.py, and tests/unit/test_git_operations.py — all three reported 'would be left unchanged'. Module follows PEP 8, 88-char line length, full type hints, and the project's docstring style.
